libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment or system-property setup is required. The current failure is caused by tests using removed org.apache.pekko.actor.TypedActor/TypedProps/TypedActorExtension APIs: these classes are present in 1.1.5 but absent from the 2.0.0-M1 actor jar. Generate/update tests around still-present classic actor APIs such as ActorSystem, Props, Actor, scheduling, dispatchers, config, serialization helpers, or other APIs available in the resolved 2.0.0-M1 artifact. Pekko documentation lists pekko-testkit as a conventional test dependency, but it is optional for meaningful coverage because actor behavior can be exercised with plain actors plus futures/latches.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8742 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: If the generated tests intentionally use Pekko TestKit APIs, they will need org.apache.pekko:pekko-testkit_2.13:2.0.0-M1 as an additional test dependency, but this is not required by the library itself.

If tests target DNS resolver internals similar to the existing DnsSettingsTest, module export flags such as --add-exports=java.base/sun.net.dns=ALL-UNNAMED may be needed; ordinary actor-system coverage does not require this.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No required setup beyond the existing Pekko actor artifact is needed for meaningful coverage. The resolved org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1 POM only adds scala-library and com.typesafe:config transitively, and the classic ActorSystem/Props APIs are usable without Docker, external services, environment variables, or system properties.
